# Provider Support Reference

<!-- Generated by `cd engine && go run ./cmd/supportdoc -write`. Do not edit by hand. -->

Piper is a deterministic local approximation. Hosted CI remains the final source of truth.

Statuses: `supported-local`, `emulated`, `partial`, `validation-only`, `unsupported`, and `requires-consent`.

Runtime dispositions: `execute`, `emulate`, `inspect-only`, `reject`, and `consent`.

## Common behavior

| Category / feature | Status | Runtime | Local behavior | Hosted difference / fallback |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <a id="common-job-dependency-graph"></a>Core — Job dependency graph (`common.graph`) | `supported-local` | `execute` | Piper builds a deterministic graph and rejects missing dependencies and cycles. | Provider-specific scheduling semantics are represented only where explicitly documented. Fallback: Correct missing or cyclic dependencies. |
| <a id="common-workflow-discovery-and-yaml-parsing"></a>Core — Workflow discovery and YAML parsing (`common.workflow.discovery`) | `supported-local` | `execute` | Piper discovers and parses recognized local pipeline files. | Hosted providers may discover additional configuration through repository settings or remote composition. Fallback: Use a documented provider filename and valid YAML. |
| <a id="common-local-runner-approximation"></a>Runtime — Local runner approximation (`common.local-runner`) | `partial` | `execute` | Jobs run in local Linux Docker containers selected by Piper. | Hosted images, tool caches, operating systems, identity, and machine configuration are not reproduced. Fallback: Perform final validation on the hosted provider. |
| <a id="common-service-containers"></a>Runtime — Service containers (`common.services`) | `emulated` | `emulate` | Services run on an isolated per-job Docker network with health/startup checks. | Provider networking, DNS, option parsing, and lifecycle details can differ. Fallback: Use explicit images, aliases, health checks, and local-safe ports. |
| <a id="common-shell-step-execution"></a>Runtime — Shell step execution (`common.shell`) | `partial` | `execute` | Shell steps execute in the selected job container using Bash or supported PowerShell handling. | Shell defaults, installed tools, profiles, and operating-system behavior can differ. Fallback: Choose a compatible image and explicit supported shell. |
| <a id="common-local-artifacts"></a>Storage — Local artifacts (`common.artifacts`) | `emulated` | `emulate` | Artifacts are copied into Piper-managed local storage with workspace path checks. | Cloud retention, permissions, compression, naming, and cross-run behavior are not reproduced. Fallback: Inspect or export the local artifact and verify hosted behavior separately. |
| <a id="common-local-caches"></a>Storage — Local caches (`common.caches`) | `emulated` | `emulate` | Caches use Piper-managed per-repository local storage. | Provider scope, immutability, eviction, versioning, and backend behavior differ. Fallback: Clear local caches or verify cache behavior on hosted CI. |
| <a id="common-empty-or-unknown-executable-step"></a>Validation — Empty or unknown executable step (`common.empty-step`) | `unsupported` | `reject` | A step without a recognized executable form is rejected with a structured support error. | A hosted provider may reject the YAML earlier or support another step type. Fallback: Use a supported run, action, or task form. |

## GitHub Actions

| Category / feature | Status | Runtime | Local behavior | Hosted difference / fallback |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <a id="github-artifact-and-cache-actions"></a>Actions — Artifact and cache actions (`github.builtin-storage-action`) | `emulated` | `emulate` | Supported inputs use Piper-managed local artifact and cache stores. | Hosted backend behavior and many action inputs are not reproduced. Fallback: Use simple names, keys, and paths, then verify hosted behavior. |
| <a id="github-actions-checkout"></a>Actions — actions/checkout (`github.checkout`) | `emulated` | `emulate` | Checkout is an explicit successful local emulation because the prepared repository is already mounted. | Fetch depth, ref selection, submodules, credentials, clean behavior, and sparse checkout are not reproduced. Fallback: Prepare the desired local revision before running Piper. |
| <a id="github-local-github-actions"></a>Actions — Local GitHub actions (`github.local-action`) | `partial` | `execute` | Supported local JavaScript, composite run steps, and docker:// actions execute locally. | Nested uses steps, Dockerfile builds, action state, and full command protocol are incomplete. Fallback: Use supported runtimes and run-only composite steps. |
| <a id="github-remote-github-actions"></a>Actions — Remote GitHub actions (`github.remote-action`) | `requires-consent` | `consent` | Remote actions require explicit consent, resolve to a commit, are cached, and emit provenance. | GitHub's download service, token context, and runner command protocol are not reproduced. Fallback: Pin a commit SHA, review the source, or replace it with a local action. |
| <a id="github-setup-node-and-setup-dotnet"></a>Actions — setup-node and setup-dotnet (`github.setup-runtime`) | `emulated` | `emulate` | A compatible Node or .NET Docker image is selected when one version can represent the job. | Tool cache, authentication, architecture, caching, and multi-runtime images are not reproduced. Fallback: Use one concrete supported runtime version per job. |
| <a id="github-unsupported-github-action-runtime"></a>Actions — Unsupported GitHub action runtime (`github.unsupported-action`) | `unsupported` | `reject` | The action fails with a structured support error and is never reported successful. | GitHub may support the action or runtime. Fallback: Use a supported action, local command, or hosted CI. |
| <a id="github-reusable-workflows"></a>Composition — Reusable workflows (`github.reusable-workflow`) | `unsupported` | `reject` | Reusable workflow execution is rejected before local job steps begin. | GitHub resolves inputs, secrets, nested calls, permissions, and outputs. Fallback: Run the called workflow directly or validate the caller on GitHub. |
| <a id="github-environment-approval"></a>Deployment — Environment approval (`github.environment`) | `emulated` | `emulate` | Environment jobs pause for explicit local approval and use restricted networking. | GitHub environment protection rules, reviewers, secrets, and deployment records are not reproduced. Fallback: Use non-production credentials and validate protections on GitHub. |
| <a id="github-workflow-triggers"></a>Events — Workflow triggers (`github.triggers`) | `validation-only` | `inspect-only` | Triggers are displayed; the user chooses a local event name and inputs. | Piper does not decide whether GitHub would start a workflow. Fallback: Provide local event/input values and verify actual triggering on GitHub. |
| <a id="github-job-and-step-if-conditions"></a>Expressions — Job and step if conditions (`github.conditions`) | `partial` | `execute` | A documented subset of status, comparison, boolean, string, context, and output expressions is evaluated. | GitHub coercion, functions, object semantics, and context completeness are not fully reproduced. Fallback: Simplify the condition or validate unsupported syntax on GitHub. |
| <a id="github-expression-interpolation"></a>Expressions — Expression interpolation (`github.interpolation`) | `partial` | `execute` | Supported expressions are interpolated immediately before their local use. | Not every GitHub field or expression construct is evaluated. Fallback: Use supported contexts or explicit local environment values. |
| <a id="github-continue-on-error"></a>Failure — continue-on-error (`github.continue-on-error`) | `partial` | `execute` | A failed step may continue the local job and emits an explicit continuation event. | Expression forms and GitHub outcome/conclusion distinctions are incomplete. Fallback: Use literal booleans and inspect step events. |
| <a id="github-permissions-and-concurrency"></a>Policy — Permissions and concurrency (`github.permissions-concurrency`) | `validation-only` | `inspect-only` | The configuration is reported but does not alter local credentials or scheduling. | GitHub token permissions, concurrency groups, and cancellation are not reproduced. Fallback: Review the values and validate policy behavior on GitHub. |
| <a id="github-defaults-run"></a>Runtime — defaults.run (`github.defaults`) | `partial` | `execute` | Working-directory defaults are applied; supported shell selection is honored where available. | Workflow-level defaults and full GitHub shell templates are not reproduced. Fallback: Declare explicit supported step working directories and shells. |
| <a id="github-job-container"></a>Runtime — Job container (`github.job-container`) | `unsupported` | `reject` | GitHub job-container semantics are rejected rather than silently replaced. | GitHub configures container credentials, options, volumes, networking, and shell behavior. Fallback: Use Piper's selected job image or validate the container job on GitHub. |
| <a id="github-runs-on-labels"></a>Runtime — runs-on labels (`github.runner`) | `validation-only` | `inspect-only` | Requested labels are displayed but do not select a hosted runner image. | GitHub label matching and hosted operating systems are not reproduced. Fallback: Select a compatible local image and validate platform-specific jobs on GitHub. |
| <a id="github-job-services"></a>Runtime — Job services (`github.services`) | `emulated` | `emulate` | Supported service fields use Piper's local service-container emulation; options are displayed but not applied. | GitHub service option parsing and networking differ. Fallback: Avoid relying on unsupported container options. |
| <a id="github-workflow-and-job-metadata"></a>Syntax — Workflow and job metadata (`github.metadata`) | `supported-local` | `execute` | Metadata is represented in the model, graph, and UI. | Display formatting may differ from GitHub. Fallback: Use standard GitHub workflow metadata. |
| <a id="github-strategy-matrix"></a>Topology — Strategy matrix (`github.matrix`) | `partial` | `execute` | Static combinations expand deterministically into selectable local job instances. | Dynamic expressions and every include/exclude semantic are not guaranteed identical. Fallback: Keep matrices static and below the configured expansion limit. |
| <a id="github-unknown-github-workflow-feature"></a>Validation — Unknown GitHub workflow feature (`github.unknown`) | `unsupported` | `reject` | Unknown executable semantics are surfaced and rejected. | The key may be valid GitHub syntax not yet modeled by Piper. Fallback: Consult the support reference or validate the feature on GitHub. |
| <a id="github-workflow-job-and-step-env"></a>Variables — Workflow, job, and step env (`github.env`) | `partial` | `execute` | Environment maps are merged and supported expressions are interpolated before execution. | GitHub coercion, context availability, and all environment-file semantics are incomplete. Fallback: Use literal values or supported expressions and avoid relying on unavailable contexts. |
| <a id="github-step-and-job-outputs"></a>Variables — Step and job outputs (`github.outputs`) | `partial` | `execute` | Single-line and multiline step outputs feed steps and needs contexts; job outputs are interpolated after execution. | All environment-file commands, output limits, encoding, and edge cases are incomplete. Fallback: Use GITHUB_OUTPUT with simple values and verify complex cases on GitHub. |

## GitLab CI/CD

| Category / feature | Status | Runtime | Local behavior | Hosted difference / fallback |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <a id="gitlab-child-and-multi-project-pipelines"></a>Composition — Child and multi-project pipelines (`gitlab.child-pipeline`) | `unsupported` | `reject` | Triggered pipeline behavior is rejected locally. | GitLab can create child or remote project pipelines with provider-side permissions. Fallback: Open a local child file directly or validate the trigger on GitLab. |
| <a id="gitlab-default-inheritance"></a>Composition — default inheritance (`gitlab.default`) | `partial` | `execute` | The default image is inherited; other default fields are reported as incomplete. | GitLab supports broader default inheritance and inherit controls. Fallback: Declare critical fields directly on jobs. |
| <a id="gitlab-extends-and-hidden-jobs"></a>Composition — extends and hidden jobs (`gitlab.extends`) | `supported-local` | `execute` | Jobs are resolved through reverse deep merging with cycle detection. | Every inheritance control and GitLab merge nuance is not yet covered. Fallback: Use acyclic local templates and inspect the resolved YAML. |
| <a id="gitlab-local-includes"></a>Composition — Local includes (`gitlab.include-local`) | `supported-local` | `execute` | Local include files are recursively merged with cycle detection before parsing. | All GitLab include merge nuances and integrity metadata are not guaranteed. Fallback: Keep local include graphs small and acyclic. |
| <a id="gitlab-remote-project-and-template-includes"></a>Composition — Remote, project, and template includes (`gitlab.include-remote`) | `unsupported` | `reject` | Piper does not fetch them and rejects execution because the resolved pipeline is incomplete. | GitLab fetches and merges remote/project/template content under provider trust rules. Fallback: Vendor the include locally or validate on GitLab. |
| <a id="gitlab-environments-and-manual-jobs"></a>Deployment — Environments and manual jobs (`gitlab.environment-manual`) | `emulated` | `emulate` | Piper pauses for explicit local approval and restricts deployment-job networking. | GitLab protected environments, deployment records, and permissions are not reproduced. Fallback: Use non-production credentials and verify protections on GitLab. |
| <a id="gitlab-workflow-rules"></a>Events — workflow: rules (`gitlab.workflow-rules`) | `validation-only` | `inspect-only` | Workflow rules are reported but do not decide whether the local pipeline exists. | GitLab can suppress or configure the entire pipeline before jobs are created. Fallback: Choose a local event explicitly and validate pipeline creation on GitLab. |
| <a id="gitlab-job-rules-only-and-except"></a>Expressions — Job rules, only, and except (`gitlab.rules`) | `partial` | `execute` | A documented subset is converted to a local job condition. | Rule ordering, attributes, variable expansion, exists, compare_to, and many ref forms are incomplete. Fallback: Use simple explicit conditions and verify complex rules on GitLab. |
| <a id="gitlab-allow-failure"></a>Failure — allow_failure (`gitlab.allow-failure`) | `partial` | `execute` | A failed local job can be converted to a successful scheduler result with an explicit event. | Exit-code forms and GitLab warning status semantics are incomplete. Fallback: Use literal booleans and inspect job_failure_allowed events. |
| <a id="gitlab-dependencies-resource-group-coverage-retry-and-timeout"></a>Failure — dependencies, resource_group, coverage, retry, and timeout (`gitlab.unsupported-job-policy`) | `unsupported` | `reject` | Execution is rejected instead of silently ignoring job data flow, locking, reporting, retry, or timeout behavior. | GitLab implements each feature in its runner and scheduler. Fallback: Remove the feature for a local-only fixture or validate the job on GitLab. |
| <a id="gitlab-images-and-runner-tags"></a>Runtime — Images and runner tags (`gitlab.image-tags`) | `partial` | `execute` | The selected image is used locally; tags are display metadata. | Runner selection, entrypoints, credentials, and executor behavior differ. Fallback: Use a Bash-capable trusted image. |
| <a id="gitlab-script-before-script-and-after-script"></a>Runtime — script, before_script, and after_script (`gitlab.scripts`) | `partial` | `execute` | Script blocks become ordered Bash steps; after_script currently follows normal success-gated step behavior. | GitLab after_script isolation, timeout, failure, and cancellation semantics are incomplete. Fallback: Keep local scripts Bash-compatible and verify after_script edge cases on GitLab. |
| <a id="gitlab-services"></a>Runtime — Services (`gitlab.services`) | `emulated` | `emulate` | Services use Piper's local service-container network. | GitLab executor networking and service command/entrypoint behavior differ. Fallback: Use explicit aliases and trusted images. |
| <a id="gitlab-artifacts-and-cache"></a>Storage — Artifacts and cache (`gitlab.artifacts-cache`) | `emulated` | `emulate` | Common declarations use Piper-managed local stores. | Reports, exclusions, expiry enforcement, scopes, and cloud backends differ. Fallback: Use simple paths and inspect local records. |
| <a id="gitlab-jobs-stages-and-needs"></a>Syntax — Jobs, stages, and needs (`gitlab.metadata`) | `partial` | `execute` | Jobs and explicit needs are normalized; missing needs use an adjacent-stage approximation. | GitLab stage scheduling, optional needs, and all DAG forms are not fully reproduced. Fallback: Prefer explicit simple needs for deterministic local behavior. |
| <a id="gitlab-parallel-jobs-and-matrix"></a>Topology — parallel jobs and matrix (`gitlab.parallel`) | `unsupported` | `reject` | Parallel expansion is rejected before execution. | GitLab creates numbered or matrix job instances and maps dependencies accordingly. Fallback: Create explicit local jobs or validate on GitLab. |
| <a id="gitlab-unknown-gitlab-job-feature"></a>Validation — Unknown GitLab job feature (`gitlab.unknown`) | `unsupported` | `reject` | Unknown semantics are surfaced and rejected. | The key may be valid GitLab syntax not yet modeled. Fallback: Consult the support reference or validate on GitLab. |
| <a id="gitlab-global-and-job-variables"></a>Variables — Global and job variables (`gitlab.variables`) | `partial` | `execute` | Variables are merged into the local job environment. | Recursive expansion, protected/masked variables, file variables, and full precedence are incomplete. Fallback: Use explicit literal variables and verify precedence on GitLab. |

## Azure Pipelines

| Category / feature | Status | Runtime | Local behavior | Hosted difference / fallback |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <a id="azure-parameters-and-compile-time-expressions"></a>Composition — Parameters and compile-time expressions (`azure.parameters`) | `unsupported` | `reject` | Execution is rejected because parameterized configuration is not resolved. | Azure expands typed parameters and compile-time template expressions before runtime. Fallback: Create a resolved local fixture or validate on Azure. |
| <a id="azure-repository-pipeline-package-and-container-resources"></a>Composition — Repository, pipeline, package, and container resources (`azure.resources`) | `unsupported` | `reject` | Execution is rejected because referenced resources are not resolved. | Azure authenticates and fetches configured external resources. Fallback: Vendor required local content or validate on Azure. |
| <a id="azure-templates-and-root-extends"></a>Composition — Templates and root extends (`azure.templates`) | `unsupported` | `reject` | Execution is rejected because templates are not expanded. | Azure resolves nested step, job, stage, variable, and extends templates. Fallback: Create a resolved local fixture or validate on Azure. |
| <a id="azure-deployment-jobs"></a>Deployment — Deployment jobs (`azure.deployment`) | `emulated` | `emulate` | Deployment jobs use a local approval gate and internal-only network. | Azure environments, checks, service connections, strategies, and deployment history are not reproduced. Fallback: Use non-production credentials and validate deployment controls on Azure. |
| <a id="azure-triggers-prs-and-schedules"></a>Events — Triggers, PRs, and schedules (`azure.triggers`) | `validation-only` | `inspect-only` | Trigger metadata is displayed; the user supplies a local build reason. | Piper does not decide whether Azure would queue a pipeline. Fallback: Select a local event and verify queueing behavior on Azure. |
| <a id="azure-job-and-step-condition"></a>Expressions — Job and step condition (`azure.conditions`) | `partial` | `execute` | A subset of status, equality, and boolean functions is evaluated. | Compile-time expressions, all runtime functions, dependency outputs, and type coercion are incomplete. Fallback: Use supported condition functions or verify on Azure. |
| <a id="azure-continueonerror"></a>Failure — continueOnError (`azure.continue-on-error`) | `partial` | `execute` | Step and job failures can continue locally and emit explicit continuation/allowed-failure events. | Azure result states and expression forms are incomplete. Fallback: Use literal booleans and inspect events. |
| <a id="azure-timeoutinminutes-and-cancellation-timeout"></a>Failure — timeoutInMinutes and cancellation timeout (`azure.timeout`) | `unsupported` | `reject` | Provider timeout declarations are rejected because only Piper's explicit local timeout settings are enforced. | Azure applies job and cancellation timeouts with agent semantics. Fallback: Configure Piper's local timeout explicitly and verify provider timeout behavior on Azure. |
| <a id="azure-checkout-step"></a>Runtime — checkout step (`azure.checkout`) | `emulated` | `emulate` | Checkout is an explicit successful local emulation because the prepared repository is already mounted. | Repository resources, clean, fetch, submodules, credentials, and persistence are not reproduced. Fallback: Prepare the desired local revision before running Piper. |
| <a id="azure-job-container"></a>Runtime — Job container (`azure.job-container`) | `unsupported` | `reject` | Azure job-container semantics are rejected. | Azure configures container resources, endpoints, options, mounts, and agent initialization. Fallback: Use Piper's selected job image or validate on Azure. |
| <a id="azure-pool-and-vmimage"></a>Runtime — pool and vmImage (`azure.pool`) | `validation-only` | `inspect-only` | The requested pool/image is displayed but does not select a Microsoft-hosted image. | Azure agent demands, capabilities, operating systems, and hosted images are not reproduced. Fallback: Choose a compatible local image and validate platform-specific work on Azure. |
| <a id="azure-bash-script-cmdline-and-powershell"></a>Runtime — Bash, script, CmdLine, and PowerShell (`azure.scripts`) | `partial` | `execute` | Scripts run through Bash or pwsh when available; CmdLine is approximated with Bash. | Windows cmd.exe, task wrappers, profiles, preferences, and agent logging commands differ. Fallback: Use explicit Bash or ensure pwsh exists in the image. |
| <a id="azure-service-containers"></a>Runtime — Service containers (`azure.services`) | `emulated` | `emulate` | Services use Piper's local per-job Docker network. | Azure container resources, endpoints, options, and agent networking differ. Fallback: Use trusted images and simple service configuration. |
| <a id="azure-workingdirectory"></a>Runtime — workingDirectory (`azure.working-directory`) | `supported-local` | `execute` | Relative working directories are resolved below the prepared workspace. | Azure task-specific path defaults may differ. Fallback: Use repository-relative paths. |
| <a id="azure-pipelines-stages-jobs-and-dependencies"></a>Syntax — Pipelines, stages, jobs, and dependencies (`azure.metadata`) | `partial` | `execute` | Azure structures are normalized into jobs and deterministic dependency edges. | Stage conditions, approvals, server jobs, and all dependency expressions are incomplete. Fallback: Use agent jobs with explicit dependencies. |
| <a id="azure-script-and-runtime-setup-tasks"></a>Tasks — Script and runtime setup tasks (`azure.task-runtime`) | `partial` | `execute` | Known tasks map to local shell execution or Node image selection. | Task versions, inputs, tool caches, logging commands, and platform behavior are incomplete. Fallback: Use documented inputs or replace the task with an explicit local command. |
| <a id="azure-artifact-and-cache-tasks"></a>Tasks — Artifact and cache tasks (`azure.task-storage`) | `emulated` | `emulate` | Known handlers use Piper-managed artifact and cache stores. | Pipeline artifact services, scopes, retention, and many task inputs are not reproduced. Fallback: Use simple names, keys, and paths, then validate hosted behavior. |
| <a id="azure-unknown-azure-task"></a>Tasks — Unknown Azure task (`azure.unknown-task`) | `unsupported` | `reject` | Unknown tasks fail with a structured support error. | Azure may provide the task through its marketplace or built-in catalog. Fallback: Use a supported task or replace it with an explicit local command. |
| <a id="azure-matrix-strategy"></a>Topology — Matrix strategy (`azure.matrix`) | `partial` | `execute` | Named legs expand deterministically into local job instances. | Dynamic strategy expressions and every Azure scheduling nuance are incomplete. Fallback: Keep matrix legs static and below the expansion limit. |
| <a id="azure-unknown-azure-pipeline-feature"></a>Validation — Unknown Azure pipeline feature (`azure.unknown`) | `unsupported` | `reject` | Unknown semantics are surfaced and rejected. | The key may be valid Azure syntax not yet modeled. Fallback: Consult the support reference or validate on Azure. |
| <a id="azure-root-stage-job-and-step-variables"></a>Variables — Root, stage, job, and step variables (`azure.variables`) | `partial` | `execute` | Literal variables are merged and supported macros/expressions are evaluated at local runtime. | Variable groups, secret variables, precedence, compile-time insertion, and logging commands are incomplete. Fallback: Use explicit local values and verify complex precedence on Azure. |
